ops.title: Prompt-cache port to v14.6: two non-obvious adaptations
ops.body:
Porting the prompt-cache restructure (JUDGE/AUDIT/REFINE prompt split into SYSTEM+USER, cached_judge) from wfa onto v14.6 main required two semantic adaptations vs the literal wfa diff:

1. cached_judge internals: wfa authored it against RunSpec/run/Result(parsed) directly. v14.6 structured_judge instead routes through spawnllm.extract(). The wfa RunSpec path IS still compatible with spawnllm 0.6.2 (extract itself builds RunSpec(response_model=...) then run(spec, backend=backend) and returns resp.result.parsed), so cached_judge ports near-verbatim. One change: inline provider_configs={"claude": ClaudeConfig(system_prompt=system)} into the RunSpec call (matching run_claude's pattern) instead of hoisting to a bare local — a hoisted local infers dict[str, ClaudeConfig], not assignable to RunSpec.provider_configs' dict[ProviderName, ProviderConfig] under Linux ty; the inline literal type-checks via expected-type inference.

2. Fixture regenerated, not deleted: wfa diff literally DELETES tests/fixtures/build_prompt_2_0.txt, but the byte-fixture test (test_build_prompt_matches_the_byte_fixture) survives in wfa's test_triage.py and still imports FIXTURE_PATH+render. wfa's working copy is a mid-flight state. Correct semantic port: change render() to JUDGE_USER/AUDIT_USER then regenerate via `python -m tests.fixture_prompt`. Main's fixture was 732 lines (v14.6 rendering, diverged from wfa parent's 498); regenerated to 234 lines (context blocks only — the static instruction preamble moved to the system prompt, which build_prompt/the byte fixture never covered). Net git effect on main: a MODIFICATION (mostly deletions), not a deletion.

Invariant (E19.B NULL, JUDGE content must stay exactly v6): verified byte-for-byte — SYSTEM + "\n\n" + USER reconstructs each original prompt exactly (JUDGE 7655ch, AUDIT 4312ch, REFINE 2225ch), and every {placeholder} lives only in USER blocks. No wording drift.

Enrich-isolation hunks already landed on main as 328f82f — only test_enrich.py's structured_judge→cached_judge seed-helper renames were cache-related and still needed.
